title: Fluent 2 — AI Harm
summary: Fluent 2 product-design framing for AI harms, categorising harm types, severity, product-responsibility boundaries, and the design decisions required before high-impact automation ships.
lessons: Require an explicit harm model and severity-weighted test cases before any high-impact automation feature ships.
Distinguish AI harm categories (e.g., unfair outcomes, privacy, reliability, safety) as separate design and QA concerns, not a single 'risk' checkbox.
Link harm assessment outcomes to rule.ai.ux-failure-contract, rule.ai.ux-agency-contract, and rule.ai.ux-uncertainty-contract so each harm type maps to a concrete design response.
caveats: The page carries no visible update date and may lag behind current Fluent releases.
Harm taxonomy reflects Microsoft's product context; apply principles, do not import Microsoft-specific severity thresholds without revalidation.
licenseNote: Public first-party Fluent documentation; cite and synthesize.
antiImitationNote: Adopt harm-model discipline and severity-weighted testing practices; do not import Microsoft product-specific harm classifications or brand materials.
